name: Pork Chops and Fried Rice
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Sliced green onions with tops
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, bring the water to a boil (add more water if needed). Stir in rice and cook for 20 minutes.
2. In a skillet, cook bacon until crisp. Remove bacon and crumble, reserving 3 tablespoons of drippings. Set aside.
3. In a bowl, combine eggs and 2 tablespoons of water. Cook the eggs in 1 tablespoon of reserved drippings in the same skillet. Add the drained rice, crumbled bacon, sliced green onions, and 3 tablespoons of soy sauce to the eggs. Mix well.
4. Turn the mixture into a greased 1 1/2 quart casserole dish.
5. In another skillet, brown the pork chops in 1 tablespoon of drippings.
6. Arrange the chops atop the rice mixture and brush with additional soy sauce.
7. Bake, tightly covered, at 350 degrees for about 40 minutes or until chops are tender.
